target_role: Technical Support Analyst - Operations (m/f/d), evening shift
job_posting: Korial follow-the-sun support model. Evening shift 4 PM - 12 AM CST + weekends. HARD requirements: 'Confident working with Linux-based systems via the command line (CLI). Ability to connect to remote systems using SSH and follow troubleshooting runbooks. Comfortable inspecting logs and performing basic system checks in a terminal environment.' Optional: Spanish fluency 'is a significant advantage', ITIL Foundation, Zendesk/Jira Service Management familiarity. Remote US, evening hours, weekend rotation.

rationale.claude: Strong evening-shift / independent-work narrative and good metric anchor (75% wait reduction). But the resume CATASTROPHICALLY overclaims Linux scope when the JD makes Linux CLI a hard requirement. Summary L3 'Linux server administration', bullet L12 'Administered Linux Photon servers', skills line 'Linux Server Administration' — all contradict career_master.md §3 L181 which caps Linux exposure at 'installing RustDesk for remote access. That is the entire scope.' If interviewer probes 'walk me through inspecting /var/log/syslog' the candidate fails technical screen. Also missing JD-explicit signals: no Spanish surfaced despite JD calling it a 'significant advantage', no Jira Service Management / Zendesk familiarity even at the soft level, no robotics-adjacency framing from the BTB Arduino/DMX/laser systems. Drops to nearmiss because the foundation is correct (real Tier 1-2 help desk experience, real documentation discipline) but the surface claims would not survive screening.

key_strengths: Summary opens with role-appropriate framing (Technical Support Analyst candidate, evening-shift fit)
Real metric anchor: 75% guest transition reduction (10 min → 2.5 min)
Soft skills list correctly emphasizes independent work, calm methodical problem-solving, reliability in non-standard hours — directly mirrors JD wording
key_weaknesses: Three Linux overclaim instances (summary + bullet + skills line) — all contradict career_master.md §3 L181 explicit limit
Skills list 'iOS/Android Mobile Support' — contradicts career_master.md §3 L179 (incidental only)
Cover letter doesn't mention Spanish bilingual ability despite JD listing it as 'significant advantage' (Carlos is fluent — PR-born)
No Zendesk / Jira Service Management mention even at familiarity level — JD lists both as 'helpful additional qualifications'
Recurring double em-dash heading pattern: 'Technical Operations Manager — IT & Live Production — BEAT THE BOMB'
specific_fixes: Replace all 'Linux Server X' claims with 'Linux CLI (basic; follows runbooks); production exposure limited to RustDesk install on game-backend host (per career_master.md §3 L181). Currently extending via home-lab practice.'
Drop 'iOS/Android Mobile Support' from skills entirely
Add 'Jira Service Management (familiarity)' + 'Zendesk (familiarity)' to skills — cheap signal that JD vocabulary registered
Add Spanish to cover letter with explicit JD-callout language
Add 'ITIL-aligned incident management (no formal certification)' to skills as honest framing of BTB workflow
tier_definition_match: nearmiss: foundation and structure are right but specific surface claims contradict honest-limits AND fail to capture explicit JD signals. Would screen out at technical phone interview when the Linux CLI question arrives. As of 2026-06-02 the integrity_guards.py strip_honest_limit_violations() gate would now auto-drop the Linux Server claims at build time — so this exact failure mode is structurally prevented going forward.
